title: Paphia sclerophylla
intro: Paphia sclerophylla, commonly known as the Paphia sclerophylla plant, is a type of orchid native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its purported health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Paphia sclerophylla include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Paphia sclerophylla include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory infections.
